channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: 2 women tell Ethics panel Gaetz paid them for sex, attorney says
item.description: Two women testified before the House Ethics Committee that former Rep. Matt Gaetz (R-Fla.) paid them to have sex, according to the attorney representing them.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 2 are killed and 1 is critically wounded in New York City knife rampage. A suspect is in custody
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A man stabbed three people across a swath of Manhattan on Monday morning, killing two and critically wounding the third without uttering a word to his victims, officials said.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pennsylvania's high court orders counties not to count disputed ballots in US Senate race
item.description: HARRISBURG, Pa. (AP) — Pennsylvania's state Supreme Court on Monday weighed in on a flashpoint amid ongoing vote counting in the U.S. Senate election between Democratic Sen.
item.dc:creator: MARC LEVY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Steve Bannon's trial in a border wall charity scheme case delayed until February
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Steve Bannon 's border wall fundraising trial was postponed to February as prosecutors disclosed Monday that the former Trump White House strategist himself once suggested the nonprofit venture was “a scam.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: India overtakes China as top source of international students at US colleges
item.description: India has overtaken China as the top source of international students for U.S. colleges, according to a report from Open Doors.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Former officer sentenced for beating a man with a baton 45 times in Los Angeles
item.description: A former police officer with the Veterans Affairs Police Department (VAPD) has been sentenced for hitting an unarmed man dozens of times with a baton, officials announced Friday.
item.dc:creator: Lily Dallow, Fox 5

item.title: About 20% of Americans regularly get their news from influencers on social media, report says
item.description: About one in five Americans – and a virtually identical share of Republicans and Democrats – regularly get their news from digital influencers who are more likely to be found on the social media platform X, according to a report released Monday by the Pew Research Center.
item.dc:creator: Haleluya Hadero, WFLA, NBC

item.title: New York teen uses passion for bowling, engineering to inspire others
item.description: “As a little kid, seeing all these huge machines, just to set up bowling pins, it's like ‘Wow,’" said Joe Dayer, known as “Joe the Pro.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Minkewicz, NewsNation

item.title: Big rigs getting stuck on tiny North Carolina mountain roads after I-40 closure
item.description: "If GPS apps send you to other routes, don't try it," transportation officials said. "Doing so may lead to a ticket, getting stuck or even overturning.
item.dc:creator: Rodney Overton, NewsNation

item.title: Should women be allowed to fight on the front lines? Trump's defense pick reignites debate
item.description: President Donald Trump’s nominee for defense secretary, Pete Hegseth, has reignited a debate that many thought had been long settled.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Daniel Penny trial resumes with third day of testimony from medical examiner, questions over cause of death
item.description: Dr. Cynthia Harris returned to the witness stand for her third day of testimony Monday in the trial against Daniel Penny for chokehold that killed Jordan Neely.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Neo-Nazi march in Ohio draws rebuke from Biden
item.description: President Joe Biden joined the chorus of state and city officials who are condemning a weekend neo-Nazi march in Columbus, Ohio.
item.dc:creator: Brian Hofmann, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trial begins for Florida man in human smuggling case after freezing deaths of Indian family at Canada-US border
item.description: FERGUS FALLS, Minn. (AP) — Federal prosecutors plan to argue at a Minnesota trial that two men were part of a criminal network stretching from India to Canada that smuggled families seeking better lives in the United States.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL GOLDBERG Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Student protesters face potential Trump crackdown
item.description: Those on campus have already seen stricter rules around demonstrations this academic year, but President-elect Trump, as a candidate, has threatened protesters with everything from military action to deportation.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KRON4

item.title: Triathlete with terminal cancer completes Ironman championship in Hawaii
item.description: An Ironman is a test of endurance featuring a 2.4-mile swim, a 26.2-mile marathon run, and a 112-mile bike ride.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, ABC 4

item.title: The 9 GOP senators who could derail Trump’s Cabinet picks
item.description: Trump can afford three defections within the Senate GOP conference and still get his picks confirmed.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, Fox 5

item.title: Veteran news executive Reg Murphy, who survived abduction decades ago, has died at 90
item.description: Reg Murphy, a renowned journalist whose newsgathering career included stints as an editor and top executive at newspapers in Atlanta, San Francisco and Baltimore — and who found himself the subject of national headlines when he survived a politically motivated kidnapping — has died at age 90.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SCHREINER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: An Israeli strike in Beirut kills Hezbollah's spokesman, while a strike in Gaza kills at least 30
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — A rare Israeli strike in central Beirut killed the Hezbollah militant group's chief spokesman on Sunday, while an Israeli strike in northern Gaza ’s Beit Lahiya killed at least 30 people, a hospital director there told The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: FADI TAWIL, BASSEM MROUE and WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Biden authorizes Ukraine to use US-supplied long-range missiles for deeper strikes inside Russia
item.description: President Joe Biden has authorized the use of U.S.-supplied long-range missiles by Ukraine to strike even deeper inside Russia, the latest easing of limitations meant to prevent the conflict from further spiraling, according to one U.S. official and three people familiar with the matter.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: A third November storm, Sara, serves notice that a busy hurricane season isn't over yet
item.description: SAVANNAH, Ga. (AP) — As the third named storm to emerge during November, Tropical Storm Sara serves as a reminder that the Atlantic hurricane season hasn't quite ended.
item.dc:creator: RUSS BYNUM, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Biden becomes the first sitting US president to visit the Amazon rainforest
item.description: Joe Biden on Sunday became the first sitting American president to set foot in the Amazon rainforest, as the incoming Trump administration seems poised to scale back the U.S. commitment to combating climate change.
item.dc:creator: KRON4

item.title: Will heating bills be higher in Utah this winter? It may be the opposite, report says
item.description: Last year's winter was the warmest on record, which helped keep energy costs low. Long-range forecasts indicate the winter of 2024-2025 is expected to be colder, especially in northern states and the Midwest, which would hike up gas and electricity bills.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, ABC 4

item.title: Will heating bills be higher this winter? It depends on where you live
item.description: Last year's winter was the warmest on record, which helped keep energy costs low. Long-range forecasts indicate the winter of 2024-2025 is expected to be colder, especially in northern states and the Midwest, which would hike up gas and electricity bills.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, Fox 5

item.title: How this duct-taped banana came to be worth $1 million
item.description: Walk into any supermarket and you can generally buy a banana for less than $1. But a banana duct-taped to a wall? That might sell for more than $1 million at an upcoming auction.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Whoopi Goldberg called to apologize for saying bakery denied order over politics
item.description: New York City politicians are calling on Whoopi Goldberg to apologize for suggesting on air that a local bakery had declined to make “The View” co-host a batch of desserts for her birthday because of her political beliefs.
item.dc:creator: Philip Marcelo, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump attends UFC championship fight in New York, taking break from Cabinet picks
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump walked out to a roaring standing ovation just ahead of the start of the UFC pay-per-view card at Madison Square Garden on Saturday night, combining two things close to his heart: fierce battles inside the octagon and New York City.
item.dc:creator: Will Weissert, WFLA, NBC

item.title: California to remove racist term for Native American Woman from more than 30 places
item.description: California will remove the derogatory term for a Native American woman in more than 30 locations across the state, according to the state Natural Resources Agency.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: SEE IT: Two New York State Patrol cars smashed by alleged drunk driver
item.description: Two New York State Police cruisers were struck by an alleged drunk driver while the officers were investigating an incident, police said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Jake Paul believes he can fight for a title, but Mike Tyson might be done in the ring at 58
item.description: ARLINGTON, Texas (AP) — Jake Paul believes he can fight for a championship belt within two years. Mike Tyson might be finished in the ring after the 58-year-old former heavyweight champion's first sanctioned pro bout since 2005.
item.dc:creator: SCHUYLER DIXON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: South Carolina tween threatened to 'shoot up the school' if Mike Tyson lost fight, authorities say
item.description: Sheriff Carter Weaver said the Georgetown Middle School student told investigators she was joking, and no firearms were found during the search of a home.
item.dc:creator: Adam Benson, WGN 9

item.title: Draymond Green's foul against Zach Edey is upgraded by the NBA to a flagrant 1
item.description: Golden State forward Draymond Green's foul against Memphis' Zach Edey in the third quarter of the Warriors' victory over the Grizzlies has been upgraded to a category-one flagrant, the NBA said Saturday after reviewing the play.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Bullet strikes Southwest Airlines flight in Dallas
item.description: The Texas incident comes just days after JetBlue Airways, Spirit Airlines and American Airlines halted flights to Haiti after two aircraft were hit by bullets amid the gang violence escalating in the country.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WGN 9

item.title: Strike by workers at a casino near the Las Vegas Strip enters 2nd day
item.description: A walkout by hundreds of hospitality workers at a casino near the Las Vegas Strip entered its second day with union members willing to undergo the financial hardships of being out of work as they wait for a new contract.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: The first Applebee's restaurant had a wildly different name
item.description: If you think “Applebee’s Neighborhood Grill and Bar” is a mouthful, just wait until you hear what the restaurant was originally called.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, WFLA, NBC

item.title: McDonald's is investing $100 million to bring customers back after E. coli outbreak
item.description: McDonald's is investing $100 million to bring customers back to stores after an outbreak of E. coli food poisoning tied to onions on the fast-food giant's Quarter Pounder hamburgers.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Betty White Forever: New stamp will honor the much-beloved 'Golden Girls' actor
item.description: The United States Postal Service might have found a way to unite a nation bitterly divided after this month's election: It's releasing a Betty White stamp.
item.dc:creator: BRENDAN FARRINGTON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Why you should throw away your black plastic cooking utensils
item.description: The tools you cook with may be introducing an unwanted ingredient into your family dinner: flame retardant.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, WGN 9
